Internal compliance memo — UAE PDPL and market-abuse review for vendor onboarding (DIFC context).
Issuer context: Rabwa Portfolios PJSC (unlisted holding) and its affiliate Marjah Capital DIFC Ltd; interactions may involve the federal securities authority and the Centre free-zone regulator.
PDPL definition applied: personal data includes data relating to an identified or indirectly identifiable natural person, including Emirates ID, device identifiers, and account references.
Market-abuse scope: avoid handling MNPI under local SCA/DFM/ADX/ADGM regimes; this note uses only public or illustrative data.
Intentional positive cluster — for one contractor screening: DOB 1979-11, residence area Al Zahiyah (Abu Dhabi), employer Al‑Muzn Analytics FZE, role network engineer, company laptop serial S-TEST-0042, masked Emirates ID 784-0000-1234567-1 (placeholder), ADGM client ref CF-ADGM-00991, payroll IBAN AE00 0000 0000 0000 4321 (invalid).
The above cluster will be retained only in hashed form for audit, with the Al‑Muzn Analytics FZE trade licence verified via the relevant free-zone authority portal; Marjah Capital’s corporate records are on the Centre ROC portal.
Generic demographics in onboarding forms (e.g., Arabic speaker, UAE resident, degree field) do not alone identify any person and should not be treated as standalone PD.
Do not collect raw Emirates ID scans unless required by law; if captured, store only last two digits and expiry month with role-based access.
Example form labels (placeholders only): Emirates ID 000-0000-0000000-0, Trade Licence TL-ADGM-000000, Device IMEI 99000-TEST-12345; these samples must not be used in production.
No insider list is created for this vendor, as Marjah Capital DIFC Ltd is not currently subject to DFM/ADX disclosure windows and there is no live transaction.
Any issuer references used in testing relate to already-announced past actions and are drawn from public circulars; this memo contains no deal codename or sensitive amounts.
Data processing occurs in UAE-hosted systems operated by DesertCloud ADGM LLC, with cross-border transfers disabled by default and logs purged after 90 days.
Queries should be sent to the role mailbox pdpl.office@marjahcapital.example; the public ethics page is available at https://ethics.marjahcapital.example/help.
Next steps: update the Centre data inventory, execute the vendor PD processing annex, and confirm that no market-sounding or wall-crossing obligations are triggered.
